tasks.name: TASK-13.1: Create HeroParallax.tsx Island component
tasks.description: Create React Island component at src/components/hero/HeroParallax.tsx that renders SVG graph visualization with requestAnimationFrame-based mouse tracking. Component updates 7 CSS custom properties (--mx, --my, --mx-pct, --my-pct, --mx-px, --my-px, --m-deg) with 0.10 lerp damping factor. Implements mousemove/mouseleave event handlers. Includes prefers-reduced-motion detection to skip animation on accessibility mode. useEffect hook manages RAF loop setup and cleanup. Export as default. TypeScript strict mode, no any types.
tasks.notes: CSS var defaults: --mx: 0, --my: 0, --mx-pct: 0%, --my-pct: 0%, --mx-px: 0px, --my-px: 0px, --m-deg: 0deg. Lerp formula ensures smooth damping without jank. RAF loop cancellation critical for cleanup on unmount. No global state mutation.

relatedFiles.description: React Island component with parallax tracking logic

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create file at src/components/hero/HeroParallax.tsx
2. Define interface HeroParallaxProps (empty for now, can add SVG dimensions later)
3. Implement useEffect hook that:
   - Checks window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches on mount
   - If reduced motion: set all 7 CSS vars to defaults and skip RAF
   - If animation enabled: start RAF loop that runs on every frame
4. Implement mousemove listener that captures event.clientX/clientY
5. In RAF callback, calculate lerped values using 0.10 factor: newVal = currentVal + (targetVal - currentVal) * 0.10
6. Update document.documentElement.style with all 7 CSS custom properties
7. Implement mouseleave listener that resets all values to defaults (0 or 0deg)
8. Return cleanup function that cancels RAF and removes listeners
9. Render SVG graph (placeholder 200x200px for now, can be enhanced later)
10. Add style block with CSS vars declared :root or component scoped
11. Verify no console.log or debug code
12. Export as named export for Island client:idle loading in HeroSection.astro
tasks.verificationCriteria: Component renders without error. requestAnimationFrame is properly cancelled on unmount (check React DevTools Profiler). CSS vars update smoothly on mousemove without jank (60fps). prefers-reduced-motion=reduce disables animation (test via DevTools emulation). No console.log or debug statements. TypeScript strict mode passes (tsc --noEmit). Linter clean (ESLint).
tasks.analysisResult: Phase 1 implements three interactive features for the Astro blog (TASK-13, TASK-14, TASK-15) by leveraging Phase 0 completions. All tasks integrate with existing components (HeroSection, Header, ListItem, TagCloud) and utilities (searchPosts). No architectural conflicts or premature abstractions. Design follows established patterns: Astro Islands with client:idle, CSS custom properties, container queries, TypeScript strict mode, zod validation.
tasks.summary: HeroParallax.tsx created with full RAF-based parallax implementation. Component correctly implements 7 CSS custom properties (--mx, --my, --mx-pct, --my-pct, --mx-px, --my-px, --m-deg) with 0.10 lerp damping factor. Includes prefers-reduced-motion detection for accessibility. SVG tree placeholder renders correctly. All event listeners properly cleaned up on unmount.

tasks.name: TASK-13.2: Integrate HeroParallax into HeroSection.astro
tasks.description: Modify src/components/hero/HeroSection.astro to mount HeroParallax.tsx as client:idle Island inside hero container. Position HeroParallax absolutely behind text content using z-index layering. Update hero styles to accommodate Island rendering. Verify HeroParallax receives correct container dimensions and mouse event coordinates.
tasks.notes: client:idle ensures HeroParallax loads after page interactive, not blocking LCP. Z-index layering critical: parallax must render behind text. Test on both desktop and mobile viewports.

relatedFiles.description: Add HeroParallax Island import and mount with client:idle, update styles for z-index layering

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Open src/components/hero/HeroSection.astro
2. Import HeroParallax component: import HeroParallax from './HeroParallax'
3. Add <HeroParallax client:idle /> inside .hero__inner div, positioned behind text
4. Update .hero CSS to set position: relative and establish stacking context
5. Style HeroParallax wrapper to position: absolute, inset: 0, z-index: 0
6. Ensure text content gets z-index: 1 for layering
7. Add container/wrapper div around HeroParallax if needed for positioning
8. Test that mouse movement triggers parallax effect when Island loads
9. Verify mobile layout: HeroParallax should be hidden on small screens or scale appropriately
10. Check that scroll and parallax don't conflict
tasks.verificationCriteria: HeroSection renders with HeroParallax Island. Mouse movement triggers parallax effect (visual inspection). No layout shift or FOUC. Mobile view correct (no parallax jank on small screens). Build succeeds (npm run build). Astro linter clean.
tasks.analysisResult: Phase 1 implements three interactive features for the Astro blog (TASK-13, TASK-14, TASK-15) by leveraging Phase 0 completions. All tasks integrate with existing components (HeroSection, Header, ListItem, TagCloud) and utilities (searchPosts). No architectural conflicts or premature abstractions. Design follows established patterns: Astro Islands with client:idle, CSS custom properties, container queries, TypeScript strict mode, zod validation.
tasks.summary: HeroParallax Island successfully integrated into HeroSection.astro with proper z-index layering (parallax z-index: 0, text z-index: 1). Component renders without errors with client:idle directive for non-blocking loading. Build passes (10 pages built in 4.26s). CSS vars (--mx, --my, --mx-pct, --my-pct, --mx-px, --my-px, --m-deg) ready for mouse tracking. SVG tree graphic renders correctly. RAF loop and event listener cleanup properly implemented. Mobile layout accommodates Island rendering.

tasks.name: TASK-14.1: Create SearchOverlay.tsx Island component
tasks.description: Create React Island component at src/components/ui/SearchOverlay.tsx that implements Cmd+K search modal. Renders full-viewport blur overlay with centered 600px max-width modal. Integrates searchPosts utility for score-based search. Manages localStorage recent searches (max 5 items). Implements keyboard navigation (↑↓ arrow keys to navigate results, Enter to select, Esc to close). Marks search term in results with HTML <mark> tag. Exports as Island for client:idle loading.
tasks.notes: localStorage key: 'blog-recent-searches', format: JSON.stringify(string[]). Mark highlighting can use simple string replace or regex. Recent searches show when input empty. Results filtered by searchPosts score > 0. Selected result highlighted with blue background. Keyboard nav essential for accessibility.

relatedFiles.description: React Island component for Cmd+K modal with search and keyboard navigation

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create file at src/components/ui/SearchOverlay.tsx
2. Define interface SearchOverlayProps (receives onClose callback, allPosts: BlogPost[])
3. Implement useState for: isOpen, query, results, selectedIndex, recentSearches
4. Implement useEffect that:
   - Loads recentSearches from localStorage on mount (key: 'blog-recent-searches')
   - Parses JSON array, defaults to empty array
5. Implement keydown listener for:
   - 'Escape': close modal (setIsOpen(false))
   - 'ArrowUp': selectedIndex-- (clamp to 0)
   - 'ArrowDown': selectedIndex++ (clamp to results.length-1)
   - 'Enter': navigate to selected result (window.location.href = ...)
   - '/' (when modal closed): open modal (setIsOpen(true))
6. Implement input onChange handler that calls searchPosts(allPosts, query)
7. Implement recent search click handler that: sets query, executes search, saves to localStorage
8. Implement saveSearch function: add query to recentSearches array, keep max 5, stringify to localStorage
9. Create renderResults function that applies <mark>query</mark> tags to excerpt (use highlightText helper)
10. Render full-viewport modal: overlay div with blur, centered 600px modal container
11. Modal contains: input field, recent searches list, results list
12. Results show: title, category badge, excerpt with mark, date, tags
13. Add keyboard shortcuts hint (display '⌘K / Ctrl+K / /')
14. Style with CSS container queries (no @media width queries)
15. Export as default for Island mounting
tasks.verificationCriteria: SearchOverlay renders on Cmd+K keypress. Input field accepts text and filters results. Recent searches persist to localStorage. Keyboard navigation (↑↓ Enter Esc) works correctly. Search term marked in results with <mark> tag. No console.log. TypeScript strict. Build passes. Linter clean. Mobile keyboard responsive.
tasks.analysisResult: Phase 1 implements three interactive features for the Astro blog (TASK-13, TASK-14, TASK-15) by leveraging Phase 0 completions. All tasks integrate with existing components (HeroSection, Header, ListItem, TagCloud) and utilities (searchPosts). No architectural conflicts or premature abstractions. Design follows established patterns: Astro Islands with client:idle, CSS custom properties, container queries, TypeScript strict mode, zod validation.

tasks.name: TASK-14.2: Wire SearchOverlay Island to Header.astro
tasks.description: Modify src/components/layout/Header.astro to mount SearchOverlay.tsx Island and wire Cmd+K button to trigger modal open. Update Header script to dispatch custom event or call Island directly when search button clicked. Ensure Island loads with client:idle and receives allPosts collection.
tasks.notes: allPosts must be fetched in Header frontmatter and passed as prop to SearchOverlay Island. client:idle ensures modal loads deferred. Custom event approach: header dispatches 'open-search', Island listens and responds. Simpler: pass onOpen callback.

relatedFiles.description: Import SearchOverlay Island, fetch allPosts, wire Cmd+K button to modal trigger

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Open src/components/layout/Header.astro
2. Add import for SearchOverlay: import SearchOverlay from '../ui/SearchOverlay'
3. Get allPosts collection inside Header frontmatter: const allPosts = await getCollection('blog')
4. Add <SearchOverlay client:idle allPosts={allPosts} /> before </header> closing tag
5. Update Header script keydown listener (lines 52-57) to focus SearchOverlay input instead of clicking button
6. Alternatively: dispatch custom event from header that SearchOverlay listens for
7. Verify allPosts prop passes correctly from getCollection to Island
8. Test that Cmd+K, Ctrl+K, and '/' all trigger modal
9. Test modal close with Esc key
10. Verify search functionality works end-to-end
tasks.verificationCriteria: SearchOverlay Island mounts in Header. Cmd+K/Ctrl+K/slash opens modal. Modal closes with Esc. allPosts prop flows correctly. Search results display. Build succeeds. No TypeScript errors. Header still renders correctly. Mobile responsive.

tasks.name: TASK-15.1: Create search.astro page with server-side rendering
tasks.description: Create Astro page at src/pages/search.astro that renders search results as SSG. Parses ?q= searchParams from URL, executes searchPosts utility, displays results with mark highlighting. Shows TagCloud empty state when no results found. Reuses ListItem component for consistent styling. Implements container query responsive design (no @media width queries).
tasks.notes: SSG page: renders at build time for each unique ?q= param. highlightText replaces instances of query with <mark>query</mark>. TagCloud empty state shows when no query or no results. ListItem reused directly, no modification. Container queries ensure mobile-first responsive design.

relatedFiles.description: SSG page for search results with mark highlighting and empty state

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create file at src/pages/search.astro
2. In frontmatter:
   - Import BaseLayout, Header, Footer
   - Import ListItem, TagCloud components
   - Import searchPosts utility
   - Import getCollection from 'astro:content'
   - Get allPosts = await getCollection('blog')
   - Get searchParams: const query = Astro.url.searchParams.get('q') || ''
   - Execute search: const results = query ? searchPosts(allPosts, query) : []
3. In template:
   - Render BaseLayout with title 'Search Results'
   - Add Header component
   - Main section with:
     - Search input field (prefilled with query value)
     - Results count display: 'X results for "query"'
     - If results.length > 0: render ListItem for each result with highlighting
     - If results.length === 0 and query: show 'No results. Try different keywords.'
     - If results.length === 0 and !query: show TagCloud from all posts
   - Add Footer component
4. Implement highlightText function or use regex to mark query in excerpts
5. Style with container queries: @container app (max-width: Npx) pattern
6. Add back link to blog list
7. Test navigation from search modal results to this page
8. Verify ?q= param parsed correctly
tasks.verificationCriteria: Page renders at /search?q=test. Results display correctly with mark highlighting. Empty state shows TagCloud. ListItem styled consistently. Build succeeds (npm run build). No TypeScript errors. Mobile responsive (container queries). Navigating from SearchOverlay to results works. Back navigation to blog list works.

relatedFiles.description: Add CSS styles for search results layout, mobile optimization, mark highlighting
